content.problemStatement:
Chef gives you 2 strings AAA and BBB. You can perform the following operation on AAA as many times as you want.


Remove character AiA_{i}AiŌĆŗ from the string AAA. This has a cost of iii which is the index of the element that you are removing.
**Note that cost is always equal to the index in current string and not the original string.** Refer to the sample test case for example.

You have to print the minimum cost of transforming AAA to BBB using the above operation. If it is not possible to transform string AAA to BBB, print ŌłÆ1-1ŌłÆ1.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of 2 lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains string AAA.
The next line contains string BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum cost of transforming string AAA to BBB. If it is not possible to transform string AAA to string BBB using above operation, print ŌłÆ1-1ŌłÆ1.
constraints: 1ŌēżŌłŻAŌłŻŌēż2Ōŗģ1051 \leq |A| \leq 2 \cdot 10^51ŌēżŌłŻAŌłŻŌēż2Ōŗģ105 (ŌłŻAŌłŻ|A|ŌłŻAŌłŻ refers to length of string AAA)
The sum of ŌłŻAŌłŻ|A|ŌłŻAŌłŻ and ŌłŻBŌłŻ|B|ŌłŻBŌłŻ over all test cases won't exceed 2Ōŗģ1052 \cdot 10^52Ōŗģ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:

Remove A1A_{1}A1ŌĆŗ which is the character a with cost of 111, AAA becomes ccd.
Remove A1A_1A1ŌĆŗ which is the character c with cost of 1, AAA becomes cd which is equal to BBB.
Total cost is 222.

Test Case 2:

It is not possible to transform string AAA to BBB using the operation.

Test Case 3:

Remove A1A_{1}A1ŌĆŗ with cost 1. Now, AAA is aaabbb.
Remove A1A_{1}A1ŌĆŗ with cost 1. Now, AAA is aabbb.
Remove A3A_{3}A3ŌĆŗ with cost 3. Now, AAA is aabb.
Remove A3A_{3}A3ŌĆŗ with cost 3. Now, AAA is aab.
Remove A3A_{3}A3ŌĆŗ with cost 3. Now, AAA is aa which is equal to BBB.
Total cost is 111111.
